articles.title: Israel claims killing of Hamas military commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad
articles.summary: Israel claims killing of Hamas military commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said Israeli forces had killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, described by Israel as Hamas’s top military commander in Gaza. “We assassinated [Izz al-Din] al-Haddad, commander of Hamas’ armed wing in the Gaza Strip,” Netanyahu and Katz said in a joint statement published by Israeli media. The statement said al-Haddad was one of “the architects” of the Hamas-led attacks on Israel on 7 October 2023. Hamas had not issued an immediate response to the anno

articles.title: UN says Israeli military obstructed peacekeepers in south Lebanon
articles.summary: UN says Israeli military obstructed peacekeepers in south Lebanon United Nations deputy spokesperson Farhan Haq said the Israeli military obstructed UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon on two occasions on Thursday. Speaking to reporters, Haq said an Israeli tank stopped peacekeepers escorting the Lebanese Red Cross south of al-Bayada and pointed its main gun at the convoy before ordering it to leave the area. “The convoy was later able to proceed,” he said. In a separate incident, Haq said another Israeli tank “briefly” blocked a United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) patrol near Naq

articles.title: Israel says it targeted senior Hamas military leader in Gaza
articles.summary: Israel says it targeted senior Hamas military leader in Gaza Israel said it carried out a strike in Gaza targeting Izz al-Din al-Haddad, whom it described as the most senior Hamas military leader remaining in the Gaza Strip. There was no immediate information on al-Haddad’s condition, and Hamas has not commented on the claim. Israel has accused al-Haddad of being one of the “architects” of the Hamas-led attacks on southern Israel on 7 October 2023 and placed him on its most-wanted list.

articles.title: European markets fall as Iran war fuel inflation fears
articles.summary: European markets fall as Iran war fuel inflation fears European stock markets closed sharply lower on Wednesday as investor concerns over inflation and geopolitical instability deepened, following talks between US President Donald Trump and Chinese Premier Xi Jinping that failed to ease tensions surrounding the US-Israeli war on Iran. Paris closed down 1.60 percent, Frankfurt fell 2.07 percent, London dropped 1.71 percent and Milan lost 1.87 percent as markets reacted to continued uncertainty over the conflict and its economic impact.

articles.title: Israeli strikes hit ambulance centre in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Israeli strikes hit ambulance centre in southern Lebanon Israeli warplanes carried out two air strikes on the town of Harouf in southern Lebanon, destroying an ambulance centre linked to the Islamic Health Authority and wounding at least two people. Lebanon’s National News Agency said paramedics were among those injured after the strikes hit an emergency response facility in the Nabatieh area. According to the United Nations, at least 103 Lebanese medical workers have been killed and 230 injured in more than 130 Israeli attacks since the war with Hezbollah resumed on 2 March.

articles.title: Boeing Gets China Deal at Trump’s Visit, With Many Questions
articles.summary: Boeing Co. appears to have secured its long-awaited order from China during US President Donald Trump’s visit to the country, but whatever accord was reached remained nebulous on numbers, aircraft type and a sense of timing. Sid Philip, Bloomberg Chief Correspondent for Global Aviation, discusses the lack of context around the agreement as well as Trump's pronouncement that Beijing might dramatically expand its initial commitment. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US has redirected 75 commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz as of today
articles.summary: US has redirected 75 commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz as of today The United States has redirected 75 commercial vessels and disabled four others to date, as part of its ongoing blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, according to an X post by the US Central Command. The Central Command also revealed that over 20 US warships are maintaining the blockade on the Strait.
